sections.paragraph:
i.q. סָלָא and סָלַל to lift up. Whence-

(1) To suspend a balance, to weigh, see Pual.

(2) Like the Latin elevavit, i.e. contemsit, to despise (as light things are of small importance and value, heavy things of much), Psa 119:118. (Chald. and Syr. id.)

Piel, i.q. Kal No. 2, Lam 1:15.

Pual, pass. of Kal No. 1, Job 28:16 used of wisdom, לֹא תְסֻלֶּה בְּכֶתֶם אֹופִיר “it cannot be weighed with gold of Ophir,” it cannot be bought with gold.

II.

סלה

perhaps i.q. שָׁלָה (ס and שׁ being interchanged), to be quiet, to be silent. Hence-
